name: web_help_bot
description: Public web help bot: an HTTP endpoint (POST /api/help) that anyone on the site can chat with, built from generic tasks (httpListener + httpReceiver + executeScript with the scope-bounded `pawflow` API + handleHTTPResponse). Mirrors telegram_help_bot but with HTTP ingress/egress. Each browser session (cookie) gets its own conversation with a pre-existing help agent (no relay, web tools only), a sliding TTL, a hard message->response timeout, an Origin allowlist, and a shared daily-resettable LLM budget across all conversations. Front it with Caddy (same-origin /api/help + per-IP rate limit); keep the listener port private. Deploy in USER scope: the deploying user owns every conversation and the budget variable.

http_host.description: Bind host for the internal HTTP listener (reached only by Caddy).

http_port.description: Bind port for the internal HTTP listener. Do NOT expose publicly; Caddy reverse-proxies /api/help to it.

allowed_origin.description: Required Origin header; requests with a different Origin are rejected (403). Empty = accept any origin (not recommended for a public endpoint).

max_cost.description: Global LLM spend ceiling (USD) across ALL conversations of this bot. When the running total reaches it, the endpoint returns 503 until reset. 0 = unlimited.

budget_var.description: Name of the USER-scope variable holding the running spend (USD). Visible in the Variables panel; set it to 0 there to reset the budget.

cookie_name.description: Session cookie name (HttpOnly, set by the flow) used to key each visitor's conversation.

agent_definition.description: Name of an EXISTING agent definition in the store (the PawFlow help agent).

instance_name.description: Agent instance name in each conversation (default = agent_definition).

llm_service.description: LLM service id used by the help agent.

skills.description: Comma-separated existing skill names to associate (PawFlow knowledge).

tools.description: Comma-separated tool allowlist for the help agent.

conv_ttl_seconds.description: Sliding per-session conversation TTL (re-armed on each message).

response_timeout_seconds.description: Hard max for message->response. On timeout the turn is force-cancelled.

sweep_schedule.description: CRON schedule for proactively purging expired conversations.

runtime_links.description: Submit public web messages to the shared PawFlow agent runtime
